ety: Zend śraonī, rt. śru = S. स्रु
def: s.f. The buttocks, hips, thighs.
s.f. Wild saffron; dogs-bane; Hermodactylus; Colchicum illyricum.
s.f. A cattle-plague.
s.f. Good course of conduct, good demeanour, good behaviour, good practice.
s.f. = sura-gāʼo, q.v. The Yāk of Tartary, or bushy-tailed bull (or cow) of Thibet, Bos grunniens.

s.m. = sajjan or sajan, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), Well-born, of good family, well-bred, respectable, reputable; virtuous, good; — a virtuous or good man; a respectable man, one of good family; — a sweetheart, lover; husband.

s.m. See s.v. sur, 'a god.' s.m. Sound, voice, tone, tune; harmony; music, melody, air, song; note (of the musical scale), the gamut; a bass, drone, or accompaniment to the shāhnāʼī; — air breathed through the nostrils, nasal sound or twang; snoring; — accent, tone; — a vowel
subs: s.m. (f.?), The junction or coalition of vowels; the laws of euphony as regards the junction of the vowels
vulg. sur-bidyā, s.f. The science of sound, acoustics
(-kā), To tune an instrument in harmony (with another); to sing in tune
s.m. Major key; soprano or alto
adj. & adv. Inharmonious, discordant; — out of tune
or maddham sur, s.m. The tenor; contralto
s.m. Low tone; the bass; minor key; — nīće sur-meṅ, adv. In a low tone; softly.

ety: old P. śurkh, śuhar; Pehl. śukhr; Zend śukhra; S. शुक्र
def: adj. Red (syn. lāl); high (as a price or rate); — s.f. The seed of the Abrus precatorius (syn. ghuṅgćī, q.v.)
subs: s.m. The erysipelas, St. Anthony's fire
s.f. A kind of willow or cane; — name of a medicinal plant
adj. Red in the face; honourable, unabashed, nullâ culpâ pallescens; honourably acquitted, exonerated; triumphant
s.f. Acquittal, exoneration; success; honour, character, fame
surḵẖ-o-sufaid, adj. lit. 'Red and white,' fair; ruddy; — plump, full, round
v.n. To be red; to become red (with anger, &c.), to flush; to be or become blood-shot; to be ripe (as fruit).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سرخ (surx). Doublet of شکر (śukra, “bright”).

def: adj. & s.m. = سرخه surḵẖa, q.v.

ety: prob. S. शूलकः; cf. M. सुव्वका
def: s.m. A tall and tapering tree; a tall plant running up without a due proportion of leaves.
adj. & s.m. Of a roan, or gray, or cream colour (a horse); — a roan horse; — red tincture; red wine; liquid fire; a kind of red pigeon; — name of a son of Afrāsyāb.
s.m. The ruddy-goose, Anas casarca (syn. ćakwā); — a species of lark
subs: (-meṅ), 'To wear a surḵẖāb's feather'; to be exalted, be advanced in dignity, to receive an addition of titles or honours (under the Mogul emperors the nobles of the court commonly wore a surḵẖāb's feather, whence the phrase); to be some great or wonderful man; — to do anything expeditiously
'To have a surḵẖāb's feather on the head'; to be proud, to hold one's head high, be above (one's) work.

ety: surḵẖ, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Redness; red ink; blood; brick-dust, pounded bricks; erysipelas ( = surḵẖ-bāda)
subs: adj. Inclined to red, slightly red, reddish.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سرخی. By surface analysis, سرخ + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. = سڙکي suṛkī, q.v.
s.f. The act of letting a line or string run out, the sudden slackening of the string of a paper kite.
s.f. corr. 1˚ of surḵẖī; — 2˚ of sirkī, qq.v.
s.m. = سرمه surma, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سرمه (surma).

ety: fr. Zend śrum, acc. of śru; cf. surb, 'lead'
def: s.m. Lead-ore; antimony (reduced to powder); collyrium (of antimony, or lead-ore or sulphuret of lead)
subs: adj. Stained or anointed with surma (the eyes or eyelids
s.m. or surma-dānī, vulg. surme-dānī, s.f. A box or receptacle for surma
v.t. To reduce to a state like that of surma, to reduce to an impalpable powder
s.m. One who applies, or has applied, surma to the eyes (either his own or another's); an instrument by which surma is applied; enlightener of the eye or aider of sight; — a dark night
s.f. The act of applying surma to the eyes)
v.n. To be reduced to an impalpable powder.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سرمه (surma), from Turkic sürmek ("to draw along, rub, smear") or, alternatively, from Proto-Indo-European *ḱel- (“to cover, conceal”).

def: s.m. = سرمه surma, q.v.

adj. (f. -ī), Heroic, brave, valiant, bold, &c. ( = sūr, q.v.). s.m. Sound, voice, tone, tune; harmony; music, melody, air, song; note (of the musical scale), the gamut; a bass, drone, or accompaniment to the shāhnāʼī; — air breathed through the nostrils, nasal sound or twang; snoring; — accent, tone; — a vowel
subs: s.m. (f.?), The junction or coalition of vowels; the laws of euphony as regards the junction of the vowels
vulg. sur-bidyā, s.f. The science of sound, acoustics
(-kā), To tune an instrument in harmony (with another); to sing in tune
s.m. Major key; soprano or alto
adj. & adv. Inharmonious, discordant; — out of tune
or maddham sur, s.m. The tenor; contralto
s.m. Low tone; the bass; minor key; — nīće sur-meṅ, adv. In a low tone; softly.

def: corr. 1˚ of surma; 2˚ of sūrmā, qq.v.
adj. Of surma or collyrium; of the colour of surma ( = surmaʼī).
adj. Of the colour of surma, greyish; — s.f. A dark shade of grey.
wety: From سرمہ (“kohl, soorma”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.m. Name of a musical mode (rāg).
adj. (f. -īṅ) = sūrmā, q.v.
s.m. Heroism, bravery, &c. ( = sūrtā, q.v.). adj. (f. -ī), Considerate, thoughtful, mindful, attentive, careful; accurate; intelligent, sensible, prudent.
adj. (dialec.) = sūrmā, q.v.

ety: sur = S. स्वर+nāy or nāʼe, q.v.
def: s.m. A kind of pipe; a clarion; a hautboy; — (in the Panjāb) an inflated skin (or mashk) used in crossing a river.

ety: fr. S. सृ; cf. sursur and sursurī
def: v.n. To shiver (with cold, &c.).
v.t. To call to account; to correct, punish, chastise, flog, whip.
s.m. corr. of surnā, q.v. s.m. A kind of pipe; a clarion; a hautboy; — (in the Panjāb) an inflated skin (or mashk) used in crossing a river.
s.m. A piper; clarion- (or hautboy-) player.
s.m. = surnāćī, q.v. s.m. A piper; clarion- (or hautboy-) player.
s.m. = surnā, q.v. s.m. A kind of pipe; a clarion; a hautboy; — (in the Panjāb) an inflated skin (or mashk) used in crossing a river.

ety: Pārsī śros; Pehl. śrosh; Zend śraosha, fr. śrush, rt. śru = S. श्रु
def: s.m. A voice from heaven; — an angel; the angel Gabriel; — the seventeenth day of every solar month.
wety: From Classical Persian سروش (surōš).

def: s.m. = sūp, q.v.
subs: s.f. = sūp-nakhā, q.v.s.v. sūp. s.m. A kind of winnowing basket, a kind of wicker receptacle which, when shaken about, serves as a fan for winnowing; a wooden scoop (suspended in a frame) for throwing up water for irrigation

s.f. lit. 'Having finger-nails like winnowing fans,' name of the sister of Rāvana (she fell in love with Rāma, and, being rejected by him, attacked Sītā, but was beaten off and mutilated by Lakshmaṇa; in revenge she incited her brother to carry off Sītā).

def: s.m. A winnowing basket ( = sūp, q.v.); — a measure of two droṇas
subs: s.f. lit. 'Having finger-nails like winnowing fans,' name of the sister of Rāvaṇ, q.v.

def: s.m. See s.v. sur, 'a god.' s.m. Sound, voice, tone, tune; harmony; music, melody, air, song; note (of the musical scale), the gamut; a bass, drone, or accompaniment to the shāhnāʼī; — air breathed through the nostrils, nasal sound or twang; snoring; — accent, tone; — a vowel
subs: s.m. (f.?), The junction or coalition of vowels; the laws of euphony as regards the junction of the vowels
vulg. sur-bidyā, s.f. The science of sound, acoustics
(-kā), To tune an instrument in harmony (with another); to sing in tune
s.m. Major key; soprano or alto
adj. & adv. Inharmonious, discordant; — out of tune
or maddham sur, s.m. The tenor; contralto
s.m. Low tone; the bass; minor key; — nīće sur-meṅ, adv. In a low tone; softly.

def: s.f. A small winnowing basket; a toy for children.
s.m. The Poon-tree, or Alexandrian laurel, Calophyllum inophyllum (syn. sult̤ān-ćampā).

s.m. See s.v. sur, 'a god.' s.m. Sound, voice, tone, tune; harmony; music, melody, air, song; note (of the musical scale), the gamut; a bass, drone, or accompaniment to the shāhnāʼī; — air breathed through the nostrils, nasal sound or twang; snoring; — accent, tone; — a vowel
subs: s.m. (f.?), The junction or coalition of vowels; the laws of euphony as regards the junction of the vowels
vulg. sur-bidyā, s.f. The science of sound, acoustics

s.m. Major key; soprano or alto
adj. & adv. Inharmonious, discordant; — out of tune
or maddham sur, s.m. The tenor; contralto
s.m. Low tone; the bass; minor key; — nīće sur-meṅ, adv. In a low tone; softly.

def: s.m. (dialec.), Sword-fish ( = P. sur); — a purse filled with gold or silver.

ety: for A. صرة, v.n. fr. صرّ 'to tie up,' &c.
def: s.m. A purse.

ety: prob. S. शूल + H. sā, q.v.
def: s.m. A sharp nail or spike, &c., a nail pointed at both ends.
s.f.? A wind that creeps into the joints, a piercing or nipping wind.

Creeping, crawling; rustling; — creeping sensation, titillation, irritation (produced by the crawling of ants, &c. over the body), formication; tingling, pricking (of a limb or member asleep); — hissing, whizzing; a firework, a squib; — an insect bred in grain, weevil.

s.f. corr. of sarasvatī, q.v. s.f. The wife of Brahmā, the goddess of speech and eloquence, the patroness of music and the arts, and the inventress of the Sanskrit language and the Devanāgarī character; — the river Sarsūtī (it rises in the mountains bounding the north-east part of the province of Dehlī, and, running in a south-westerly direction, becomes lost in the sands of the great desert; according to the Hindūs, however, the river only disappears in this place, and, continuing its course underground, joins the Ganges and Jamna at the junction at Allahabad); — a river (generally); — speech, eloquence; an excellent woman, an eloquent or an inventive woman; a name of Durgā; a cow.

ety: S. स्मर्त्ता; or surt, q.v. + ā = Prk. अओ = S. क+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Considerate, thoughtful, mindful, attentive, careful; accurate; intelligent, sensible, prudent.
s.f. Heroism, bravery, courage, valour, boldness, prowess
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Heroic, brave, &c.; — a hero ( = sūr, q.v.)
To display heroism or valour; to summon up courage.

def: s.f. Bravery, courage, valour, heroism, prowess

ety: surtā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Consideration, thoughtfulness, mindfulness, attention, carefulness, care; intelligence; prudence.

ety: surtā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: Consideration, thoughtfulness, mindfulness, attention, carefulness, care; intelligence; prudence.
s.f. = surtāʼī, q.v. Consideration, thoughtfulness, mindfulness, attention, carefulness, care; intelligence; prudence.

ety: surat or surt, q.v. + Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī) = surtā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Considerate, thoughtful, mindful, attentive, careful; accurate; intelligent, sensible, prudent.
s.f. A good or nice taste, or appetite, &c. (see rući).
v.t. = سڙکنا suṛuknā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Well-formed, symmetrical, handsome, beautiful, elegant; — s.m. A handsome or elegant form, fine figure, beautiful face
subs: su-rūp-vat or vant, adj. (f. -vatī), Having a handsome form, well-shaped, &c. ( = su-rūp, q.v.)

def: corr. of su-rūp, and of sva-rūp, qq.v. adj. (f. -ā), Well-formed, symmetrical, handsome, beautiful, elegant; — s.m. A handsome or elegant form, fine figure, beautiful face
subs: su-rūp-vat or vant, adj. (f. -vatī), Having a handsome form, well-shaped, &c. ( = su-rūp, q.v.)

def: s.f. Comeliness, handsomeness, elegance of form, beauty.
adj. = su-rūp, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Well-formed, symmetrical, handsome, beautiful, elegant; — s.m. A handsome or elegant form, fine figure, beautiful face
subs: su-rūp-vat or vant, adj. (f. -vatī), Having a handsome form, well-shaped, &c. ( = su-rūp, q.v.)

def: s.m. 'Making glad'; pleasure, delight, joy, cheerfulness; exhilaration (caused by wine, &c.)
subs: sarūr gaṭhnā, sarūr honā, v.n. To be exhilarated, to be flushed (with wine).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سرور (surūr), from Arabic سُرُور (surūr).

def: s.m. corr. of shorbā, q.v. s.m. Soup, broth.
s.m. A ladle in the form of a hand used to pour ghī or clarified butter on the sacrificial fire, a sacrificial ladle.
s.m. A bur, a prickly adhesive grass, Andropogon muricatum (commonly called 'love' in India).
s.f. Name of a small black shining seed (used in medicine).
s.f. The wife (or the daughter) of the Sun (Sūrya); a new bride; — a drug; the colocynth or bitter gourd.

s.m. The sun; (in Hindū poetry) a symbolical expression for the number twelve (in allusion to the sun in the twelve signs of the zodiac); — the swallow-wort, Calotropis (or Asclepias) gigantea; — name of the son of Bali
subs: s.m. The sun personified (esp. as Vishṇu)
s.m. lit. 'Son of the Sun'; an epithet of Karn; and of Sugrīv
s.f. 'Daughter of the sun,' the river Yamunā (Jamnā)
s.f. Splendour or glare of the sun
and sūrya-devatā, s.m. The sun-deity, the sun personified as a deity
s.m. Name of a celebrated Sanskrit work on astronomy (by Varāmihir)
s.f. (in Astron.), The sun's entrance into a new sign
s.m. lit. 'Sun-loved'; the sun-stone, sun-gem, crystal
s.f. (in Astron.) The course of the sun
s.f. The sun-flower, Helianthus annuus; a kind of fan or sun-shade, &c. (see sūraj-mukkhī)
s.m. The sun-stone or sun-gem ( = sūrya-kāṅt: it is supposed to yield its possessor whatever he may desire); — the shrub Hibiscus phœniceus
s.m. The sun personified (esp. as Vishṇu); a proper name
vulg. sūraj-bans, s.m. The solar race or dynasty
vulg. sūraj-bansī, adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the soiar race; — a descendant of the Sun, a person of the solar race; name of a tribe of Kshatrlyas who claim descent from the sun.
wety: From Sanskrit सूर्य (sūrya).

def: adj. Syriac; — s.f. The Syriac language.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic سُرْيَانِيّ (suryāniyy).

def: s.m. See s.v. sūrya. s.f. The wife (or the daughter) of the Sun (Sūrya); a new bride; — a drug; the colocynth or bitter gourd.
s.m. Liquorice, Glycyrrhiza.

ety: prob. S. शुंशु, or शिशुकः; or contrac. of sūs-mār, q.v.
def: s.m. The porpoise, Delphinus (also written सूइस sūʼis, in Hindī).
s.m. The noise made by a person setting on a dog
subs: or śuś karnā, v.t. To set on, to encourage or stimulate a dog to the chase by the sound śuśʼ
s.f. The impelling or setting on a dog.

def: adj. Breathing, gasping, panting.
adj. Brave, courageous, valiant.
s.f. A society.
s.m. A good or favourable witness.
s.m. 'Good news,' an epithet of the Gospel.
s.f. Good understanding or intellect, excellent judgment.
s.m. Good time, good season, period of prosperity, season of plenty.
s.m. Good news, glad tidings.
s.m. = su-sandeś, q.v. s.m. Good news, glad tidings.
s.m. = S سسنگت सुसंगति su-sangati, s.f. Good company; the association or companionship of the good.
s.f. = S سسنگ सुसंग su-saṅg, s.m. Good company; the association or companionship of the good.
s.m. Suitable companion or associate (whether for good or for evil).
part. adj. (f. -ā), Well united, auspiciously associated, well connected.
s.m. Good race or lineage, good family.
s.m. Cook, dresser and seller of provisions.
s.m. Substance, property, competence; convenience, accommodation; — a kind of jewel or crystal.
s.m. Father-in-law (either husband's or wife's father); a term of abuse pest, wretch, devil, &c.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀲𑀼𑀭 (susura), from Sanskrit श्वशुर (śvaśura), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *swáśuras, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *swáćuras, from Proto-Indo-European *swéḱuros. Doublet of خُسَر (xusar). Cognate with Punjabi سَوہرا (sauhrā), Saraiki سَوہرا (sauhrā), Sindhi سَهُرو (sahuro), Burushaski [script needed] (šūšur), Kalasha [script needed] (išpášur), Khowar اِشپاشُور (išpašúr), Torwali شأژو (š…

def: s.m. A good or faithful servant or attendant.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Well-served, much attended to.
s.m. lit. 'Having beautiful clusters'; the karaunda tree, Carissa carondas; — an epithet of Vishṇu.
adj. Dried up, dry, withered, shrivelled, &c. (see sūkhā).
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit शशक (śaśaka). Doublet of سَسَک (sasak).

def: s.f. = S ششکتو शुष्कत्व śushkatva, s.m. Dryness, shrivelledness.
s.m. = S ششکتا शुष्कता śushkatā, s.f. Dryness, shrivelledness.
part. (f. -ā), Fast asleep, sound asleep.
s.f. Deep sleep, profound repose; entire insensibility, unconsciousness.
s.f. A striped coarse cloth; (in Persian) a cloth of silk and cotton.
s.m. = su-śikshak, q.v. s.m. A good learner; a good instructor.
s.m. = su-śikshak, q.v. s.m. A good learner; a good instructor.
s.f. = su-śikshā, q.v.
s.f. Good teaching or instruction, good or sound doctrine.
s.m. A good learner; a good instructor.
adj. = سشيل su-śīl, q.v.
adj. Well-disposed, of good disposition, good-natured, good tempered; kind, affable, benevolent; of good manners, polite.
s.f. = su-śīlatā, q.v.
s.f. Goodness of disposition, excellence of temper, good-naturedness, natural amiability, kindness; politeness.
adj. = su-śīl, q.v.
adj. = su-śītal, q.v.
adj. Very cool, very cold, frigid, freezing.
s.f. Great coldness, frigidity.
s.f. Hissing, sibilating; hissing or urging on (a dog).
s.f. = H ششکارنا शुशकारना śuśkūrnā, v.t. see s.v. śuś.
v.n. To hiss (as a snake), to sibilate; — v.t. To urge (a dog) to the chase, to hiss (a dog) on.
v.t. = H ششکاري शुशकारी śuśkārī, s.f. see s.v. śuś.
s.m. The Gangetic porpoise, Delphinus gangeticus (cf. next).
(cf. H. sūsmār), s.m. lit. 'Hissing snake,' &c.; a species of Iguana (like the Lybian lizard: Hindī syn. goh); — a porpoise.
adj. Well-adorned, beautiful, handsome, comely.
adj. & s.m. Well-looking, comely, handsome; — handsomeness, comeliness.
part. Well-adorned, beautified.
s.m. Tender or auspicious grief; sympathy, compassion.
adj. Very distinct, very clear, easy, intelligible.
s.m. Father-in-law (either husband's or wife's father); a term of abuse pest, wretch, devil, &c.
adj. Of or belonging to the father-in-law's family.

s.f. Father-in-law's house or family. (The term is sometimes applied ironically to a gaol or prison, just as, in English, the pawnbroker's shop is called 'the mansion of mine uncle'; it is also used occasionally in the sense of 'no one knows where,' e.g. wǒh susrāl-ko gayā, 'he has gone nobody knows where,' 'he has made himself scarce')
subs: s.m. A son-in-law who sponges on his father-in-law.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit श्वशुरघर (śvaśuraghara), compound of श्वशुर (śvaśura, “father-in-law”) + घर (ghara, “house”), literally “father-in-law's house”.

def: s.f. = next, q.v.
adj. Distinctly audible; pleasing to the ear.
s.f. Mother-in-law; — a term of abuse.
wety: Feminine of سَسُر (sasur, “father-in-law”), inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀲𑀼𑀭 (susura), from Sanskrit श्वशुर (śváśura).

def: adj. Indisposed, heavy, languid; feeble, weak; dejected, downcast; unbraced, relaxed, lax, slack, loose; sluggish, inactive, slow, dull, depressed (as a market, &c.); indolent, slothful, idle, lazy, dilatory, tardy, negligent, remiss
subs: adj. Slow to believe, lax in confidence, incredulous, sceptical; fickle
adj. Slow or backward in fulfilling an engagement; fickle
v.t. To relax; to diminish the motion or speed of; to throw cold water on, to discourage.

def: adj. 1˚ = sust, q.v.; — 2˚ = sastā, q.v. adj. Indisposed, heavy, languid; feeble, weak; dejected, downcast; unbraced, relaxed, lax, slack, loose; sluggish, inactive, slow, dull, depressed (as a market, &c.); indolent, slothful, idle, lazy, dilatory, tardy, negligent, remiss
subs: adj. Slow to believe, lax in confidence, incredulous, sceptical; fickle
adj. Slow or backward in fulfilling an engagement; fickle
v.t. To relax; to diminish the motion or speed of; to throw cold water on, to discourage.
wety: Unknown. Compare Punjabi ਸਸਤਾ (sastā), Nepali सस्तो (sasto), Gujarati સસ્તું (sastũ), Sindhi سَستو (sasto), Bengali সস্তা (śosta), Odia ଶସ୍ତା (śastā), Assamese সস্তা (xosta), Sylheti ꠢꠍꠔꠣ (hosta), Marathi स्वस्त (svasta, hypercorrection of सस्त (sasta)).

ety: sustā˚ or sastā˚ = S. स्वस्थ+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi (caus. augment)
def: v.n. To take repose, to rest, lie down; to stop.
s.m. (?) (dialec.) A string for making images move or dance.
adj. In good condition or circumstances, well off, happy; well, healthy, in health; in comfort, or ease.
s.f. Healthiness, health; good condition, welfare, happiness, ease, comfort.
adj. Firm, steady, immovable, very still; stable, resolute, calm; cool; placid.
s.f. = S سستهرتو सुस्थिरत्व su-sthiratva, s.m. Firmness, steadiness, resoluteness, calmness.
s.m. = S سستهرتا सुस्थिरता su-sthiratā, s.f. Firmness, steadiness, resoluteness, calmness.
part. Being well or in good condition; living well; in good health; safe.

ety: sust, q.v.+Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Heaviness, languor, dulness, inactivity, depression (of a market, &c.), relaxation, feebleness; inaction, sluggishness, indolence, idleness, laziness, slowness, tardiness, dilatoriness, negligence, remissness
subs: To shake off (one's) indolence, to become alert or active; to stretch oneself
To be slothful or idle, to idle; to be dilatory, to delay.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سستی (sustī). سُسْت (sust, “lazy, idle”) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. Lukewarm, tepid.
adj. Slightly warm, lukewarm, tepid.
s.m. Good disposition, goodness of nature; — adj. Of a good disposition, good-natured, well-disposed.
adj. (f. -ī) = su-svabhāv, q.v. s.m. Good disposition, goodness of nature; — adj. Of a good disposition, good-natured, well-disposed.
s.m. Pleasant taste, delicious flavour; — adj. Well-tasted, delicious ( = su-svādu).
part. adj. Rendered pleasing to the taste, well-flavoured.
adj. Very pleasant to the taste, very sweet, delicious.
s.m. Thread, string, cord, rope, line; yarn; filament; a silver thread; wire; a tendril; (in Bot.) a stamen; an awn; a line, row; direct way, range; straightness; — one-sixteenth of a tasu (q.v.)
subs: (-meṅ or -par), To tie a string (to); to stretch a cord (for a straight line); to point (a gun, &c. at), to take aim

def: s.f. A woman recently delivered of a child, a lying-in woman.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢 (sotta), from Sanskrit *स्रोत्र (*srotra), from स्रु (sru, “to flow”).

def: s.m. (f.?) Thread, &c. ( = sūt, q.v.). s.m. Thread, string, cord, rope, line; yarn; filament; a silver thread; wire; a tendril; (in Bot.) a stamen; an awn; a line, row; direct way, range; straightness; — one-sixteenth of a tasu (q.v.)
subs: (-meṅ or -par), To tie a string (to); to stretch a cord (for a straight line); to point (a gun, &c. at), to take aim
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑁄𑀢𑁆𑀢 (sotta), from Sanskrit *स्रोत्र (*srotra), from स्रु (sru, “to flow”).

def: s.f. A female child, daughter, girl.
part. & s.m. (f. -ā), Begotten, brought forth, generated, born, produced; — a son, male child, offspring; a king, prince
subs: (˚ta+ar˚), adj. Desirous of having a son
s.m. (f. -ī), Nourisher of children; name of a class of deities
(f. -ikā), sut-ghātī (f. -inī), s.m. One who slays his son
suta-vat, sut-vant, adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Having offspring; — parent of a son

def: s.m. The sun; — quicksilver, mercury.

s.m. A charioteer, driver; a man of a mixed caste (the son of a Kshatriya father and Brāhman mother; his occupation is managing horses and driving cars); a rehearser of ancient legends, a bard, an encomiast; — a carpenter (syn. sūtra-dhār)
subs: s.m. The son of a sūta or charioteer
s.m. (f. -ī), A rehearser of ancient legends.

def: s.m. Birth, production; uncleanness or impurity from childbirth or miscarriage (also laxly applied to the impurity arising from death in a household. It attaches to all the members of a family; and the period of uncleanness varies according to the nearness of relationship, and according to local usage.)
s.m. = ستل sutal, q.v. s.m. = sutal.
s.m. = sutal.
s.f. = sutārī, q.v. s.f. An awl; a large needle, a bodkin (syn. sutālī).
s.m. (Braj) pl. (of suta or sut), Sons, children, offspring, progeny, descendants.
s.m. Good time.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ـِسْتَان (-istān), from Middle Persian -stʾn' (-estān), from Old Persian 𐎿𐎫𐎠𐎴 (s-t-a-n /⁠stāna⁠/), from Proto-Iranian *stā́nam, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *stʰáHnam, from Proto-Indo-European *steh₂- (“to stand”). Doublet of تھانَہ (thāna) and سْتھان (sthān).

def: adj. corr. of sva-tantra, q.v. adj. (f. -ā or -ī), Self-dependent, self-reliant, independent, free; self-willed, wilful; unrestrained, uncontrolled, absolute; no longer subject to parents or guardians, of age, full grown; — s.m. One who is independent or acts for himself; one who is legally entitled to act without control or guardianship
subs: (˚ra+adh˚), s.m. (f. -ī), One who is of a self-willed or despotic disposition, &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Of an unbridled, or a self-willed disposition.

def: s.m. A carpenter; wheelwright; — head workman; mate.
s.m. Time, opportune moment, opportunity (syn. ghāt).
s.m. = ستار sutār, q.v. s.m. A carpenter; wheelwright; — head workman; mate.
s.f. An awl; a large needle, a bodkin (syn. sutālī).
adj. See s.v. suta or sut. part. & s.m. (f. -ā), Begotten, brought forth, generated, born, produced; — a son, male child, offspring; a king, prince
subs: (˚ta+ar˚), adj. Desirous of having a son
s.m. (f. -ī), Nourisher of children; name of a class of deities
(f. -ikā), sut-ghātī (f. -inī), s.m. One who slays his son
suta-vat, sut-vant, adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Having offspring; — parent of a son

def: s.m. A drinker of Soma juice; an offerer of a libation (cf. sutvan).
s.m.f. Good place, pleasant spot; abode of bliss, paradise.

ety: prob. S. सूत्र्+अनं, or लः, and ल + कः
def: Drawers, trousers (sūthnā being esp. applied to those worn by men, sūthnī to those worn by women: var. forms are suttan, suttan, sūtan, &c.).
adv. (dialec.), Much, very, exceedingly, extremely.

ety: prob. S. सूत्र्+अनं, or लः, and ल + कः
def: Drawers, trousers (sūthnā being esp. applied to those worn by men, sūthnī to those worn by women: var. forms are suttan, suttan, sūtan, &c.).

r: suthnā, sutthan, suthan, suthnī, sūthan, sūthnā, sūthnī
def: s.m. = H ستهن सुत्थन sutthan, सुथन suthan, s.m. = H ستهني सुथनी suthnī, s.f. See sūthan, sūthnā, and sūthnī.
s.f. The edible root Dioscorea fasciculata.

ety: prob. S. सूत्र्+अनं, or लः, and ल + कः
def: Drawers, trousers (sūthnā being esp. applied to those worn by men, sūthnī to those worn by women: var. forms are suttan, suttan, sūtan, &c.).

r: suthnī, sutthan, suthan, suthnā, sūthan, sūthnā, sūthnī
def: s.f. = H ستهن सुत्थन sutthan, सुथन suthan, s.m. = H ستهنا सुथना suthnā, s.m. See sūthan, sūthnā, and sūthnī.

ety: prob. fr. S. सु+स्थ+र+कः; or = S. सुन्दर+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Neat, elegant, adorned, beautiful, handsome; tidy, clean; excellent, well; — suthrā or suthrā-sāh, s.m. Name of a religious mendicant
subs: vulg. suthre-sāhī, s.m. A class of mendicants who are followers of Suthrā-sāh; an individual of that class, a follower of Suthrā-sāh.

ety: suthrā, q.v. + āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Neatness, elegance, beauty, prettiness; excellence, goodness.

ety: suthrā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. = suthrāʼī, q.v. s.f. Neatness, elegance, beauty, prettiness; excellence, goodness.
adj. & s.m. Having or possessing a child, having offspring; — a father, parent.
adj. Made of cotton thread.
s.f. = sūtikā, q.v. s.f. A woman who has recently given birth to a child, a lying-in woman
subs: s.m. A room or part of a house appropriated to a woman at childbirth, a lying-in chamber.

def: s.f. Sewing, stitching.
s.f. Birth, production, delivery; child-bearing, parturition, bringing forth; offspring, progeny
subs: s.m. The month of delivery, the last month of gestation or pregnancy.

def: adj. = sutīkshṇa, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Very sharp, very pungent; very subtle; very acute, acutely painful.
adj. = sutīkshṇa, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Very sharp, very pungent; very subtle; very acute, acutely painful.
s.f. A woman who has recently given birth to a child, a lying-in woman
subs: s.m. A room or part of a house appropriated to a woman at childbirth, a lying-in chamber.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Very sharp, very pungent; very subtle; very acute, acutely painful.
s.m. See s.v. sūti. adj. & s.m. Having or possessing a child, having offspring; — a father, parent.
s.f. A mother.
s.f. A good woman; a good wife
subs: s.f. 'The gem of excellent women,' an epithet of Pārvatī; and of Sītā.

def: s.f. Tapering rod, stick, switch, cane ( = saṭak).
s.f. = suṭkun, q.v. s.f. Tapering rod, stick, switch, cane ( = saṭak).
s.m. Reins; leading rope (of a horse, &c.; syn. bāg-ḍor); — the little skin that rises backwards near the nails at the end of the fingers, hangnail.
s.f. Twine, pack-thread, string.
s.f. = ستلي sutlī, q.v. s.f. Twine, pack-thread, string.
s.m. = sutal-lok, q.v.s.v. sutal.

ety: sut˚ = Prk. सुत्त(इ) or सुत्ते(इ), fr. सुत्त = S. सुप्त, part. of स्वप्
def: v.n. To be asleep, to sleep, to lie down, repose, rest.
v.n. = sutnā, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī) = sutthan or sūthan, q.v. s.m. = H ستهنا सुथना suthnā, s.m. = H ستهني सुथनी suthnī, s.f. See sūthan, sūthnā, and sūthnī.

v.t. = sūṅtnā, q.v. v.t. To rub down; to rub (the body), to shampoo; to strip the leaves off a branch, or off vegetables, &c. (by running the hand down it or them); to strip, lay bare; to draw or unsheath (a sword); to straighten (a wire, &c., by rubbing the hand down it); to throw out, bale out (water); to drain, exhaust.

ety: fr. the trans. sūtnā or sūṅtnā
def: v.n. To become worn or emaciated, to be drawn or sunken (the cheeks, &c.).
s.m. (f. -ī) = sutthan or sūthan, q.v. s.m. = H ستهنا सुथना suthnā, s.m. = H ستهني सुथनी suthnī, s.f. See sūthan, sūthnā, and sūthnī.
adj. & s.m. Having an agreeable lisp; — one who has a pleasant lisp; one whose lispings or attempts to speak afford a sense of pleasure.
corr. of next, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = suthrā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Neat, elegant, adorned, beautiful, handsome; tidy, clean; excellent, well; — suthrā or suthrā-sāh, s.m. Name of a religious mendicant
subs: vulg. suthre-sāhī, s.m. A class of mendicants who are followers of Suthrā-sāh; an individual of that class, a follower of Suthrā-sāh.

def: s.m. Thread, &c. ( = sūt, q.v.); a collection of threads; a short rule or precept, axiom, aphorism (in morals, religion, or science); (in law) an opinion or decree; — origin
subs: (f. -ī), sūtra-dhārak (f. -ikā), sūtra-dhārī (f. -inī), sūtra-dhar (f. -ā), s.m. lit. 'Thread-holder,' a stage-manager, a director or principal actor who arranges the plot of a drama and takes a leading part in the prelude; one who (in a puppet-show) causes wooden dolls to dance; — a carpenter; the author of a set of rules or aphorisms; — an epithet of Indra; and of the Deity
s.m. The author or composer of sūtras, an author of any work containing rules or aphorisms.

def: s.m. lit. 'Good protector,' a name of Indra.
s.f. (rustic) = sutlī, q.v. s.f. Twine, pack-thread, string.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit स्त्री (strī́).

def: s.m. (f. -ī) = sutthan or sūthan, q.v. s.m. = H ستهنا सुथना suthnā, s.m. = H ستهني सुथनी suthnī, s.f. See sūthan, sūthnā, and sūthnī.

r: sutthan, suthan, suthnā, suthnī, sūthan, sūthnā, sūthnī
def: s.m. = H ستهنا सुथना suthnā, s.m. = H ستهني सुथनी suthnī, s.f. See sūthan, sūthnā, and sūthnī.
s.f. Distress, affliction, uneasiness, indigence; — adj. Sad, melancholy; indigent, helpless.

ety: Prk. सुत्तओ (with h inserted); S. शुक्तिका
def: s.f. A pearl-oyster; an oyster-shell (syn. sīpī).
v.t. To swallow by gulps and with a noise, to gulp down.

ety: Pehl. śtūn; Zend śtūna; S. स्थूणा
def: s.m. Pillar, column, prop
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سُتُون (sutūn).

ety: Pehl. śtor; Zend śtaora; S. स्थूर
def: s.m. Animal, quadruped, beast of burden, cattle; horse; camel; mule; ass.
s.f. The space between two lines; interlineation.
s.m. An offerer of Soma juice; drinker of Soma ( = suta-vat).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سُتُون (sutūn).

def: v.t. To cause to be stripped off (the leaves of a branch, or of vegetables), to cause to be made bare; to cause to be unsheathed, &c.
s.f. Bringing forth a child, parturition.

ety: for A. صعوبة, inf. n. of صعب; see ṣaʻb
def: s.f. Difficulty, hardship, trouble, distress.
s.m. Ascending; ascent; ascension; (in Alg.) involution
subs: v.t. To raise (a quantity) to a higher power
s.m. Ascent and descent; (in Alg.) involution and evolution.

def: s.m. Good speaking, elegant or chaste speech, eloquence.
s.m. Perishing, death; mortal disease; mortality; murrain.
s.f. The Indian lute; — name of several plants.

ety: dim. of saudā, fem. of aswad; see saudā
def: adj. Blackish, black; — suwaidā or suwaidāʼuʼl-qalb, s.f. The black part or grain of the heart, the heart's core; — original sin.
s.m. One who sleeps, a sleeper ( = sone-wālā).
adj. & s.m. Speaking elegantly or well, eloquent; — an elegant or eloquent speaker.
s.m. Good or excellent speech, eloquence (syn. su-vaćan).
v.t. To cause to sleep, &c. ( = sulānā, q.v.). v.t. To cause to sleep, to put to sleep; to hush, lull, quiet, calm, allay; to lay, repress, quell, subdue; to kill, murder
s.f. A sweet voice; pleasant sound or speech.

ety: = S. अश्व+वार; see aswār, and asp
def: adj. & s.m. Mounted, riding (on, -par); on horse-back; embarked (on a ship, &c.); (met.) drunk, tipsy; — one who is mounted, a rider, horseman, cavalier, horse-soldier, trooper; cavalry
subs: s.m. A horse-breaker; horseman, a good rider, a jockey
v.t. To cause to ride, to mount (one, on or in, -par or meṅ)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سوار (sawār, “rider”), from Old Persian 𐎠𐎿𐎲𐎠𐎼 (a-s-b-a-r /⁠asabāra-⁠/, “horseman”), from Proto-Iranian *Hacwabāráh, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Haćwabʰāras.

def: s.f. pl. (of ṣūrat, q.v.), Forms, shapes, figures, &c.
s.m. Excellent or beautiful water.

adj. (f. -ā), Of a good colour; bright, brilliant; golden; — of a good tribe or caste; — s.m. A good colour; good tribe or caste or class, good rank (in society); — gold, pure gold; a gold coin, a gold-mohur; a weight of gold equal to sixteen māshās; — a kind of red chalk or ochre; a species of sandal-wood; the tree Cassia fistula; a sort of sacrifice
subs: s.m. Computation of gold (i.e. of its weight and fineness); (in Arith.) allegation medial
adj. Golden; consisting of, or abounding in, gold.

def: adj. & s.m. = su-varṇ, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Of a good colour; bright, brilliant; golden; — of a good tribe or caste; — s.m. A good colour; good tribe or caste or class, good rank (in society); — gold, pure gold; a gold coin, a gold-mohur; a weight of gold equal to sixteen māshās; — a kind of red chalk or ochre; a species of sandal-wood; the tree Cassia fistula; a sort of sacrifice
subs: s.m. Computation of gold (i.e. of its weight and fineness); (in Arith.) allegation medial
adj. Golden; consisting of, or abounding in, gold.

def: s.f. Name of several plants (see su-varṇ).
s.m. A pleasant dwelling; a reputable residence; — an agreeable perfume, sweet smell, fragrance, scent; — adj. Sweet-smelling, odorous, fragrant.
adj. Dwelling in a comfortable or respectable abode; — perfumed.
s.f. A woman (married or single) residing in her father's house; a term of courtesy for a respectable woman whose husband is alive; — a perfumed woman.
s.m. A good or excellent thing.
s.m. (?), A good time, fitting season; — the sea-shore.
adj. (f. -ā), Stooping greatly, bowed; very humble, very quiet; — s.m. Name of a mountain
adj. (f. -ā), Well-dressed, well-clad; ornamented, decorated; — s.m. A good dress or guise, an ornamental dress.
adj. (f. -inī), Well-dressed, &c. ( = su-vesh, q.v.).
s.m. Good or deliberate consideration; deliberation.
adj. & adv. Of a good kind; — in a good or easy way, easily, well.
adj. corr. of su-vijna, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Very knowing or intelligent, very wise, very skilful.
part. Well-placed, well-deposited; well-furnished, well-supplied, well-appointed; well-provided; well-arranged; well-done, well-performed.
adj. (f. -ā), Very knowing or intelligent, very wise, very skilful.
wety: From سُوجْنا (sūjnā, “to swell”), from Sanskrit शूयते (śūyatē).

def: s.m. Valour, bravery, prowess.
s.m. (f. -ā), A good Brāhman, an excellent priest.
s.f. Good rest or cessation, a good end, fortunate termination.
adj. (. -ā), Very confiding; very trusty, to be depended on, confidential.
s.m. Good behaviour; good usage.
s.m. A good oblation, an excellent sacritice.
s.m. Good repute, fame, renown; — adj. Of good repute, famous, renowned.
adj. = su-yaś, q.v. s.m. Good repute, fame, renown; — adj. Of good repute, famous, renowned.
s.m. Favourable juncture, good opportunity, auspicious occasion.
s.m. ? An assignment of land for charitable purposes; a grant of land revenue without any stipulation of military service or other conditions; a feudal tenure, a fief.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Own, one's own (my own, thy own, &c., according to the context; but, in these senses, rarely used in Hindī, excepting as the first member of compounds); belonging to one's own family or tribe; proper, peculiar; innate, natural; — self, one's own self, self-identity; the soul; a kinsman, relation; belongings, property, wealth; (in Alg.) a positive or affirmative quantity.
wety: Perhaps from Sanskrit सह (saha).

def: s.m. = śvan, q.v. s.m. A dog.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Of or relating to one's own duty or religion; — a dutiful person; a religious partisan.
adj. corr. of svavaś, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Subject to one's own will, self-controlled; ruled by one's own free will, independent, headstrong; — a free agent; a headstrong person, &c.

s.m. Own state; essential or inherent property; innate or peculiar disposition; natural state or constitution; nature, character; habit, manners; fashion, custom, practice; quality, essence; peculiar purpose or aim
subs: vulg. subhāʼo-vaś, s.m. Subjection to natural disposition, &c.; — adj. (f. -ā), Under the control of the natural disposition, &c.

def: prop. svabhāv, q.v.
adj. Belonging to, or being by, one's own nature; innate, natural, native, inherent, peculiar, essential
subs: s.m. A beggar born or by nature.

def: adj. = svābhāvik, q.v. adj. Belonging to, or being by, one's own nature; innate, natural, native, inherent, peculiar, essential
subs: s.m. A beggar born or by nature.

def: s.m. lit. 'Self-existent'; the self-existent, Brahmā; Vishṇu; Śiva; — time; love ( = svayam-bhū).
s.f. One's own land, own estate; native country, fatherland.
s.f. The conceiving a thing to be one's own property.
adj. Very clear, transparent, pellucid, crystalline; white; polished; clean, pure, stainless, unsullied; sound, healthy.

s.m. One's own will, own choice or fancy; independence; ease, security, peace; — adj. Following one's own will, unrestrained, uncontrolled, independent, free; self-willed; contented, happy; spontaneous; uncultivated, wild; — adv. According to one's own will or fancy, at will; independently; voluntarily, spontaneously
subs: s.m. One who goes about at will; a Sannyāsī (q.v.).

def: s.f. = S سوچهتو स्वच्छत्व svaććhatva, s.m. Transparency, clearness; cleanness, purity; soundness, healthiness.
s.m. = S سوچهتا स्वच्छता svaććhatā, s.f. Transparency, clearness; cleanness, purity; soundness, healthiness.
s.m. Tasting, eating, drinking; relishing; taste, relish, flavour, savour; sweetness; enjoyment, pleasure
subs: (with dat. of person, and gen. of thing), To acquire a taste (for, -kā); to be addicted (to); to take interest (in)
(-kā), To try the flavour (of), to taste
(-ko), To have or get a taste (of, -kā), to relish, enjoy the flavour (of).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit स्वाद (svāda).

def: adj. = سوادو svādu, q.v. adj. Grateful to the palate, pleasant to the taste, tasty, flavoursome, savoury, sweet, delicious; dainty, delicate; pleasing, agreeable, delightful, &c.; — s.m. Sweet taste or flavour, sweetness; relish; taste; treacle, molasses; name of a medicinal plant (syn. jīvak); — s.f. A grape.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit स्वाद (svāda).

def: adj. Appertaining to flavour, &c.; of high flavour, flavoursome, delicious, tasty, palatable (syn. svādu).
adj. = svādik, and svādu, qq.v.
s.m. Own country, native country; one's own place, proper place; home
subs: s.m. and svadeśānurāgatā, s.f. (˚śa+anu˚), Passion for one's own country, patriotism
and svadeśānuragī, s.m. One who loves his native land, a patriot
s.m. Forsaking or quitting one's native country, emigration, voluntary exile
s.m. (f. -inī), One who quits his own country, an emigrant, a voluntary exile.

def: adj. Of or belonging to one's own, or to the same, country or locality; — sva-deśī, s.m. A fellow-countryman.
adj. Of or belonging to one's own, or to the same, country or locality; — sva-deśī, s.m. A fellow-countryman.
s.m. Own right; own duty, own religion; peculiar duty or occupation; the duties of one's own caste or class
subs: (˚ma+ar˚), adv. For the sake of one's own duty, or religion, or interests, &c.
s.m. One who is ready to lay down his life for duty or religion; one who has the martyr-spirit
s.m. One who is prepossessed in favour of his own religion; a religious partisan, a bigot
s.m. Neglect or dereliction of duty; abandoning one's religion, apostasy
s.f. One who is guilty of dereliction of duty; one who abandons his religion, an apostate.

def: s.m. Own office or function, peculiar authority or station.
adj. & s.m. Under the authority of self, independent; — one who is his own master.
adj. (f. -ā), Being one's own master, self-subject, self-dependent; having in one's own power; independent, uncontrolled, absolute, despotic; — s.m. (in Gram.) A neuter verb.
s.f. = S سوادهينتو स्वाधीनत्व svādhīnatva, s.m. = H سوادهينی स्वाधीनी svādhīnī, s.f. Self-dependence, independence, uncontrolledness, liberty; subjection to one's self.
s.m. = S سوادهينتا स्वाधीनता svādhīnatā, s.f. = H سوادهينی स्वाधीनी svādhīnī, s.f. Self-dependence, independence, uncontrolledness, liberty; subjection to one's self.
s.f. = S سوادهينتا स्वाधीनता svādhīnatā, s.f. = S سوادهينتو स्वाधीनत्व svādhīnatva, s.m. Self-dependence, independence, uncontrolledness, liberty; subjection to one's self.
s.m. Own supremacy; supreme sway, royalty, sovereignty.
adj. & s.m. Tasting; drinking; — taster; drinker.
adj. (super. of svādu), Sweetest; very sweet; dainty, savoury, &c.
part. Tasted, touched; relished, liked, enjoyed; flavoured, seasoned; sweetened; palatable; pleased.
s.f. One's own eye, one's own sight; personal inspection; — adv. With one's own eyes (e.g. svadr̤ishṭi dekhnā, To see with one's own eyes).

adj. Grateful to the palate, pleasant to the taste, tasty, flavoursome, savoury, sweet, delicious; dainty, delicate; pleasing, agreeable, delightful, &c.; — s.m. Sweet taste or flavour, sweetness; relish; taste; treacle, molasses; name of a medicinal plant (syn. jīvak); — s.f. A grape.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit स्वादु (svādú).

def: s.m. Welcoming, welcome; salutation.
wety: From Sanskrit स्वागत (svāgata).

def: s.f. One's own condition or course.
corr. of sva-jnāti, q.v. s.f. One's own kindred or kin; — s.m.f. Kinsman, relation.
s.f. The personification of an offering or oblation as the wife of Agni (who presides over burnt-offerings)
subs: v.t. To make a burnt-offering of; to give to the fire, to burn, to turn to ashes; to consume, to swallow or devour greedily or excessively; to make away with, to embezzle.

def: s.f. Wilfulness; independence, freedom.
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Self-willed, uncontrolled, wanton; — a self-willed person, an independent person; an adulterer.
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī) = svairī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Self-willed, uncontrolled, wanton; — a self-willed person, an independent person; an adulterer.
s.f. A wanton or unchaste woman, an adulteress.
